Validate a data frame's structure

Source: R/validators.R
dataframe_spec.Rd

Returns a validator closure that accepts data frames containing every column listed in required_cols and whose row count is within [min_rows, max_rows].

Usage

dataframe_spec(required_cols = NULL, min_rows = 0, max_rows = Inf)

Arguments

required_cols

Character vector of required column names.

min_rows

Minimum number of rows.

max_rows

Maximum number of rows (defaults to Inf).

Value

A validator function function(value) -> logical.

See also

Other validators: combine_validators(), enum_validator(), list_of(), nullable(), numeric_range(), string_length(), string_pattern(), validator_constraint(), vector_length()

Examples

is_orders <- dataframe_spec(
  required_cols = c("id", "amount"),
  min_rows = 1
)
is_orders(data.frame(id = 1:3, amount = c(10, 20, 30)))
#> [1] TRUE
is_orders(data.frame(id = integer()))
#> [1] FALSE
is_orders(data.frame(name = "Ada"))
#> [1] FALSE
